content: Lyme disease: early localized - doxycycline 100mg BID x10 days (or amoxicillin, cefuroxime); early disseminated/late - doxycycline x14-21 days; neurologic/cardiac involvement may require IV ceftriaxone 2g daily x14-28 days. Malaria (uncomplicated P. falciparum): artemether-lumefantrine (Coartem) or atovaquone-proguanil (Malarone); severe malaria: IV artesunate first-line (superior to quinine), switch to oral ACT when tolerating. P. vivax/ovale: add primaquine or tafenoquine for hypnozoite eradication (check G6PD first). Dengue: supportive care only (fluids, antipyretics, avoid NSAIDs due to bleeding risk); monitor for warning signs of severe dengue (abdominal pain, persistent vomiting, mucosal bleeding, lethargy, rising hematocrit with falling platelets). No specific antiviral therapy.
